title: Poor feeding
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Specifies suckling, bottle feeding, routines, environment, aversion, interaction, appetite, food types, and feeding-diary domains.
presenting_history.rationale: Directly supports mapped breastfeeding, formula, routines, appetite, aversion, feeding-environment, and structured-history domains.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Specifies suckling, bottle feeding, routines, environment, aversion, interaction, appetite, food types, and feeding-diary domains.

source.title: Faltering growth: recognition and management of faltering growth in children — Recommendations

investigations.text: Specifies weight and height measurement, clinical/developmental/social assessment, detailed feeding or eating history, observation, and assessment-directed investigations.
investigations.rationale: Directly supports mapped feeding history, weight or growth, measurement, observation, and assessment-directed investigation fields.
investigations.evidence_extract: Specifies weight and height measurement, clinical/developmental/social assessment, detailed feeding or eating history, observation, and assessment-directed investigations.

source.title: Faltering growth: recognition and management of faltering growth in children — Recommendations

referral.text: Defines growth monitoring and referral for underlying disorder, failure to respond, slow growth, rapid weight loss, severe undernutrition, safeguarding concern, or acute illness.
referral.rationale: Directly supports mapped weight-loss concern, monitoring, follow-up, and clinician-arranged referral documentation.
referral.evidence_extract: Defines growth monitoring and referral for underlying disorder, failure to respond, slow growth, rapid weight loss, severe undernutrition, safeguarding concern, or acute illness.

source_limitations: No exact UAE poor-feeding workflow source was identified in the reasonable authoritative search.
Acute fever, vomiting, respiratory, dehydration, urine-output, and lethargy concerns are not covered by the selected faltering-growth sections.
The source is England-specific and not directly validated for UAE practice.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
